component_identification.content: Base box includes: 7 Wonder boards (A/B sides), 7 Wonder cards, 49 Age I cards, 49 Age II cards, 50 Age III cards, 46 Conflict tokens (24 defeat -1 and 22 victory +1/+3/+5), coins (value 1 and 3), 1 score pad, 1 rulebook, and 2 quick-reference sheets. Cards cover raw resources, manufactured goods, military, science, commerce, civic points, guilds, and chaining symbols.

core_game_loop.content: The game has 3 Ages. In each Age, every player gets a hand of cards, chooses one card to play face down, then all reveal simultaneously. Played cards are either built, used to build a wonder stage, or discarded for 3 coins; then hands pass left or right depending on Age. After 6 picks, the last card is discarded. At Age end, resolve military conflicts with immediate neighbors.

detailed_rules.content: Building a card requires paying its cost in resources/coins unless you use chain symbols for free construction. Resource cards (brown/gray) produce resources each turn for future builds; yellow cards often generate coins, discounts, or trading improvements. You may buy resources from adjacent players every turn (2 coins per resource by default, modified by commerce effects), but neighbors keep producing resources regardless of your purchases. Wonder stages are built by paying the printed stage cost and tucking one selected card under your board. Science scores multiplicatively: each symbol set gives 7 points, plus square values for each symbol count. Military is not endgame-only: at each Age end, compare shield totals versus each neighbor; gain victory tokens for wins by Age (+1/+3/+5) and -1 for losses. Guild (purple) cards appear only in Age III and score based on neighbors’ cards/boards. Common errors: forgetting that each player only has two trade partners, paying for your own resources unnecessarily, and mis-scoring science sets.

scoring_and_endgame.content: After Age III military, total points from blue civic cards, wonder stages, yellow card points, purple guilds, military tokens, and science. Coins convert at 3 coins = 1 point (rounded down). Highest score wins; tie-breaker is most coins.

beginner_strategy.content: 1) Secure baseline economy in Age I (at least 2–3 reliable resource sources). 2) Watch both neighbors’ boards—your military and guild value depend on them. 3) Use chaining symbols to spike tempo in Age II/III. 4) Don’t force science without enough access; incomplete science plans underperform. 5) Build wonder stages when the draft offers weak cards, not when premium cards are available.

metadata.notes: Check 4 failed: total_token_count is 503; must be 800-3000 (hard reject if <800 or >5000)
